thesis: Adaptive systems for human state variation
problemStatement: Fixed systems fail. When human states vary (e.g., cognitive load, sensory conflict, uneven terrain), static XR, vehicles, and wearables degrade task performance and safety.
approachStatement: We build closed-loop systems that (1) measure human state, (2) adapt interface parameters, and (3) optimize explicit objectives under constraints.
handoff1to2: Sensory limits constrain Motor output (e.g., preventing motion sickness).
handoff2to3: Motor capabilities actuate Behavior tasks (e.g., real-time guidance).

layers.name: SENSORY MODELS
layers.goal: Expand user perceptual limits without breaking immersion.
layers.keyImpact: Mitigating visual-vestibular conflict and expanding operational bounds.

layers.name: MOTOR MODELS
layers.goal: Map digital actuation directly to human biomechanics and physical intent.
layers.keyImpact: Converting sensed forces into highly accurate wearable actuation (e.g., EMS).

layers.name: BEHAVIOR MODELS
layers.goal: Optimize perception-action loops in real-world tasks under cognitive/safety constraints.
layers.keyImpact: Balancing usability vs. safety dynamically (e.g., simplifying UI under heavy load).
